mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer prices indicates a healthy economic expansion without inflationary pressure, supporting higher equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy benchmark, or forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production following a contraction reflects rapid capacity utilization recovery, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew during industrial production rebounds, or the mechanism yields a negative Sharpe ratio over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding corporate hiring and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPI spikes above the threshold, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in >50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, creating a favorable environment for corporate earnings and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined retail and housing signal underperforms individual proxies, or forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of co-movement periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, capacity constraints and financing costs squeeze corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite the supply-monetary mismatch, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.
